command_template_patterns.overview: Common templates and patterns for creating effective slash commands across different use cases and domains.

template.name: simple_command
template.structure:
# filename.md
[Clear directive of what to do]
[Optional requirements or context]
template.example:
# optimize.md
Analyze the selected code for performance optimizations: - Identify inefficient algorithms - Check for unnecessary computations - Suggest caching opportunities - Recommend async improvements

template.name: command_with_description
template.structure:
--- description: [Brief description for command menu] ---
# filename.md
[Clear directive]
[Requirements]
template.example:
--- description: Analyze code for performance bottlenecks and optimizations ---
Perform a comprehensive performance analysis: - Identify O(n²) or worse time complexity - Find memory leaks or excessive allocations - Check for unnecessary database queries - Suggest caching strategies - Recommend parallelization opportunities

template.name: command_with_arguments
template.structure:
--- description: [Brief description] argument-hint: <argument-name> <arg2-name> ---
# filename.md
[Directive mentioning the expected arguments]
[How to use the arguments]
[Requirements]
template.example:
--- description: Create a new React component with TypeScript argument-hint: <component-name> ---
Create a new React component with the specified name: - Use TypeScript with proper prop types - Include JSDoc comments - Add basic styling with CSS modules - Create a Storybook story - Write unit tests with React Testing Library
Follow our project's component structure and naming conventions.

template.name: mode_targeted_command
template.description: Command that switches to a specific mode
template.structure:
--- description: [Brief description] mode: [mode-slug] ---
# filename.md
[Directive specific to the mode's capabilities]
[Requirements that leverage the mode's strengths]
template.example:
--- description: Design system architecture and component relationships mode: architect ---
Analyze the current system architecture and: 1. Document component hierarchy and relationships 2. Identify coupling and cohesion issues 3. Propose architectural improvements 4. Create architecture diagrams 5. Document design patterns in use 6. Suggest refactoring strategies

pattern.name: endpoint_generator
pattern.template:
--- description: Generate REST API endpoint with best practices argument-hint: <endpoint-name> <http-method> mode: code ---
Create a REST API endpoint with the following:
**Core Implementation:** - Route handler with proper HTTP method - Request validation using our validation library - Error handling with appropriate status codes - Response formatting following our API standards
**Security:** - Authentication middleware - Authorization checks - Input sanitization - Rate limiting consideration
**Documentation:** - OpenAPI/Swagger annotations - Example requests and responses - Error response documentation
**Testing:** - Unit tests for business logic - Integration tests for the endpoint - Test error scenarios
Follow our project's REST API conventions and patterns.

pattern.name: api_documentation
pattern.template:
--- description: Generate OpenAPI/Swagger documentation for endpoints ---
Create comprehensive API documentation for the selected endpoints:
**Document:** - HTTP method and URL path - Request parameters (path, query, body) - Request/response schemas with types - Authentication requirements - Success response examples - Error response examples with status codes - Rate limiting information
**Format:** - Use OpenAPI 3.0 specification - Include realistic example data - Add descriptions for all fields - Document validation rules
Follow our API documentation standards.

pattern.name: security_review
pattern.template:
--- description: Comprehensive security analysis and vulnerability detection ---
Perform a thorough security review of the selected code:
**Authentication & Authorization:** - Verify proper authentication checks - Check authorization before sensitive operations - Review session management - Validate token handling
**Input Validation:** - Identify potential injection points (SQL, XSS, etc.) - Check input sanitization - Verify data type validation - Review file upload handling
**Data Security:** - Check for hardcoded secrets or credentials - Verify encryption for sensitive data - Review secure communication (HTTPS) - Check password hashing methods
**Common Vulnerabilities:** - OWASP Top 10 considerations - Dependency vulnerabilities - Error message information leakage - Insecure deserialization
Provide specific remediation steps for each finding.

pattern.name: code_refactoring
pattern.template:
--- description: Identify and suggest refactoring opportunities ---
Analyze the selected code for refactoring opportunities:
**Code Smells:** - Long methods or classes - Duplicate code - God objects with too many responsibilities - Feature envy - Data clumps
**SOLID Principles:** - Single Responsibility violations - Open/Closed principle adherence - Liskov Substitution issues - Interface segregation opportunities - Dependency inversion improvements
**Suggested Refactorings:** - Extract method/class - Introduce parameter object - Replace conditional with polymorphism - Simplify complex conditionals - Improve naming clarity
For each suggestion, explain the benefit and provide a refactored example.

pattern.name: test_generator
pattern.template:
--- description: Generate unit and integration tests for code mode: code ---
Create a comprehensive test suite for the selected code:
**Unit Tests:** - Test all public methods/functions - Cover edge cases and boundary conditions - Test error handling paths - Mock external dependencies - Aim for high code coverage
**Test Organization:** - Group related tests with describe blocks - Use descriptive test names (it should...) - Follow AAA pattern (Arrange, Act, Assert) - Keep tests independent and isolated
**Test Data:** - Use meaningful test data - Create reusable fixtures when appropriate - Test with valid and invalid inputs
**Assertions:** - Verify return values - Check state changes - Validate side effects - Test error messages
Use our project's testing framework ({Jest|Mocha|Pytest|etc.}) and conventions.

pattern.name: test_coverage_analyzer
pattern.template:
--- description: Analyze test coverage and identify untested code paths ---
Review the current test coverage and identify gaps:
**Coverage Analysis:** - Identify untested functions/methods - Find uncovered code branches - Locate edge cases without tests - Check error handling coverage
**Missing Tests:** - Critical business logic paths - Error scenarios and exceptions - Edge cases and boundary conditions - Integration points - Async operation handling
**Test Recommendations:** - Prioritize tests by risk/importance - Suggest specific test cases to add - Recommend integration tests needed - Identify areas requiring E2E tests
Provide specific test case examples for high-priority gaps.

pattern.name: readme_generator
pattern.template:
--- description: Create comprehensive README for the project mode: code ---
Generate a comprehensive README.md file that includes:
**Project Overview:** - Clear project title - Brief description of purpose - Key features and benefits - Technology stack
**Getting Started:** - Prerequisites and requirements - Installation instructions (step-by-step) - Configuration setup - Quick start example
**Usage:** - Basic usage examples - API reference or CLI commands - Configuration options - Common use cases
**Development:** - Development setup - Running tests - Building the project - Contributing guidelines
**Additional Sections:** - License information - Credits and acknowledgments - Links to documentation - Contact or support information
Base the content on the current project structure and existing code.

pattern.name: function_documentation
pattern.template:
--- description: Generate comprehensive function documentation mode: code ---
Add thorough documentation to the selected functions:
**Documentation Format:** - Use appropriate doc format (JSDoc, docstring, etc.) - Clear description of what the function does - Document all parameters with types - Document return value with type - List possible exceptions/errors - Add usage examples for complex functions
**Content Guidelines:** - Explain the "why" not just the "what" - Document side effects if any - Note any performance considerations - Include links to related functions - Mention deprecation if applicable
**Examples:** - Provide realistic usage examples - Show common use patterns - Include edge case examples
Follow our project's documentation standards.

pattern.name: migration_generator
pattern.template:
--- description: Generate database migration with up and down methods mode: code ---
Create a database migration file with:
**Migration Structure:** - Clear migration name with timestamp - Up migration (apply changes) - Down migration (revert changes) - Transaction wrapping
**Schema Changes:** - Table creation/modification - Index additions - Foreign key constraints - Data type changes
**Data Migration:** - Data transformation if needed - Default value handling - Validation during migration - Rollback data changes
**Safety Checks:** - Verify migration is reversible - Check for data loss scenarios - Add appropriate constraints - Include migration comments
Follow our project's migration conventions and ORM patterns.

pattern.name: deployment_checklist
pattern.template:
--- description: Verify deployment readiness and configuration ---
Perform a comprehensive deployment readiness check:
**Code Quality:** - All tests passing - No linting errors - Code coverage meets threshold - No security vulnerabilities in dependencies
**Configuration:** - Environment variables defined - API keys and secrets configured - Database connections verified - Third-party service endpoints correct
**Documentation:** - README up to date - CHANGELOG updated - API documentation current - Deployment guide accurate
**Infrastructure:** - Database migrations ready - Static assets built and optimized - Cache strategy configured - Monitoring and logging setup
**Rollback Plan:** - Previous version tagged - Rollback procedure documented - Database migration rollback tested
Provide a checklist with status for each item.

pattern.name: multi_step_process
pattern.description: Command that guides through multiple steps
pattern.template:
--- description: [Multi-step workflow description] ---
Complete the following process step-by-step:
**Step 1: [Phase Name]** - [Action 1] - [Action 2] - Verify: [What to check]
**Step 2: [Phase Name]** - [Action 1] - [Action 2] - Verify: [What to check]
**Step 3: [Phase Name]** - [Action 1] - [Action 2] - Verify: [What to check]
**Final Verification:** - [ ] [Checklist item 1] - [ ] [Checklist item 2] - [ ] [Checklist item 3]

pattern.name: analysis_and_recommendation
pattern.template:
--- description: [Analysis description] ---
Perform analysis and provide recommendations:
**Analysis Phase:** Examine the code/system for: - [Aspect 1 to analyze] - [Aspect 2 to analyze] - [Aspect 3 to analyze]
**Findings:** Document: - Current state - Identified issues - Performance metrics - Risk assessment
**Recommendations:** Provide prioritized recommendations: 1. **High Priority:** [Critical changes] 2. **Medium Priority:** [Important improvements] 3. **Low Priority:** [Nice-to-have enhancements]
For each recommendation, include: - Rationale - Expected benefit - Implementation approach - Estimated effort

component.name: project_conventions_reference
component.description: Standard phrase to reference project patterns
component.text: Follow our project's established conventions and patterns.

variations.variation: Use our project's coding standards and style guide.
Adhere to the patterns used in similar components.
Follow the structure established in our codebase.

component.name: testing_requirement
component.text: **Testing:** - Write unit tests covering all logic paths - Include edge case tests - Test error scenarios - Ensure tests are independent and repeatable

component.name: documentation_requirement
component.text: **Documentation:** - Add code comments for complex logic - Document public APIs with JSDoc/docstrings - Update relevant README sections - Include usage examples

component.name: error_handling_requirement
component.text: **Error Handling:** - Catch and handle all possible errors - Use appropriate error types/status codes - Provide clear error messages - Log errors appropriately - Don't expose sensitive information

tip.name: project_specific_terms
tip.description: Replace generic terms with project-specific ones

examples.example: Replace "our validation library" with actual library name
Replace "our testing framework" with Jest, Mocha, etc.
Reference actual project directories or files

tip.name: tech_stack_awareness
tip.description: Tailor commands to your technology stack

examples.example: Specify Django for Python web apps
